title: Lycopodioides omeiensis
taxonomy: Lycopodioides omeiensis is a species of plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. This family is part of the order Lycopodiales, which is a group of ancient, vascular plants known as lycopods. Lycopodioides omeiensis is a type of club moss, a group of plants that have simple, needle-like leaves and produce spores for reproduction. The classification of Lycopodioides omeiensis is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its small size and limited range of distribution. Understanding the classification of Lycopodioides omeiensis is essential for botanists and researchers to study its evolution, ecology, and conservation.

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress.
